g.text: ad error.
· Do not download public sample data, GitHub CSVs, web search results, synthetic replacements, or any other external fal
lback to complete this dataset-grounded task.
· Experiment plan: 1) Define outcome and cohorts. - Outcome: viral = top 1% of engagement_rate within same calendar mont
h. engagement_rate = (retweet_count + favorite_count). Also compute normalized_engagement = engagement_rate / author_med
ian_engagement where author_median_engagement is median engagement_rate per account_id over the dataset; fallback to glo
bal median if <5 tweets per author. - Stratify by follower proxy (if follower count is unavailable, stratify by author o
verall median engagement tertiles). 2) Engineer features from full_text: - char_len, word_len - has_question (contains '
?') - has_exclaim ('!') - n_hashtags (#\w+) - n_mentions (@\w+) - has_link (http|https) - has_media proxy: presence of a
vatar_media_url is not media; so derive has_quote = quoted_tweet_id not null; has_reply = reply_to_tweet_id not null; ha
s_conversation_id not equal tweet_id; if media URLs/entities absent, skip. - starts_with_verb (simple regex against comm
on verbs) - sentiment_vader (if sentiment tool available; else skip) 3) Controls: created_at hour of day, weekday vs wee
kend, month fixed effects. 4) Analyses: - Descriptive: compare feature prevalence between viral and non-viral. - Logisti
c regression predicting viral ~ features + controls within month strata. - Partial dependence or marginal effects for ke
y features. - Robustness: Use normalized_engagement top 1% as alternate label; and top 5% as sensitivity. - Output: summ
ary table of ORs with CIs, calibrated AUC, confusion matrix at 1% threshold, and feature importance from regularized mod
el. 5) Artifacts: - CSVs: feature matrix sample (100k rows), coefficients, odds ratios; - Markdown brief with key findin
gs and guidance for crafting tweets. Implementation notes: Use efficient scanning over parquet; downsample negatives to
balance 1:10 for model training; evaluate on holdout months (last 2 months).
